intro:
There is no information about Papyrius plant, it seems you might be referring to the Papyrus plant (Cyperus papyrus) or Papyri (a paper made from papyrus plants). However, I assume you are referring to the Papyrus plant as it is a known plant. 

Here's a paragraph about the Papyrus plant (Cyperus papyrus):

The Papyrus plant (Cyperus papyrus) is a perennial aquatic plant native to Africa, known for its long, slender stems and feathery leaves. The Papyrus plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of the Papyrus plant include saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Papyrus plant include tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
